tasks.subject: [shell-level-right-panel] [P1] Create right-panel persistence hook and barrel exports
tasks.description:
Create the `useRightPanelPersistence` hook that hydrates panel state on mount, and the barrel `index.ts` for the right-panel feature module.

## Files to Create

### `apps/client/src/layers/features/right-panel/model/use-right-panel-persistence.ts`

```typescript
import { useEffect } from 'react';
import { useAppStore } from '@/layers/shared/model';

/**
 * Hydrate right panel state from localStorage on mount.
 *
 * Called once in AppShell. Restores the panel open/closed state and active tab
 * from the persisted `dorkos-right-panel-state` localStorage entry.
 *
 * This is independent of canvas persistence (which is per-session). Right panel
 * persistence tracks only structural state: whether the panel is open and which
 * tab is active.
 */
export function useRightPanelPersistence(): void {
  const loadRightPanelState = useAppStore((s) => s.loadRightPanelState);
  useEffect(() => {
    loadRightPanelState();
  }, [loadRightPanelState]);
}
```

### `apps/client/src/layers/features/right-panel/index.ts`

```typescript
/**
 * Right panel feature -- shell-level multi-occupancy panel infrastructure.
 *
 * Provides the container, tab bar, toggle button, persistence hook, and
 * keyboard shortcut for the extensible right panel in AppShell.
 *
 * @module features/right-panel
 */
export { RightPanelContainer } from './ui/RightPanelContainer';
export { RightPanelTabBar } from './ui/RightPanelTabBar';
export { useRightPanelPersistence } from './model/use-right-panel-persistence';
```

Note: `RightPanelToggle` and `useRightPanelShortcut` will be added to this barrel in Task 3.1.

## Directory Structure

After this task and 1.3/1.4, the full feature directory is:

```
apps/client/src/layers/features/right-panel/
  ui/
    RightPanelContainer.tsx   (Task 1.3)
    RightPanelTabBar.tsx      (Task 1.4)
    PanelErrorBoundary.tsx    (Task 1.3)
  model/
    use-right-panel-persistence.ts  (this task)
  __tests__/
    RightPanelContainer.test.tsx  (Task 1.3)
    RightPanelTabBar.test.tsx     (Task 1.4)
  index.ts  (this task)
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `useRightPanelPersistence` hook calls `loadRightPanelState` once on mount
- [ ] Barrel `index.ts` exports `RightPanelContainer`, `RightPanelTabBar`, and `useRightPanelPersistence`
- [ ] FSD import rules are followed: only imports from `shared/` layer
- [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly
- [ ] All existing tests continue to pass
tasks.activeForm: Creating right-panel persistence hook and barrel exports

tasks.subject: [shell-level-right-panel] [P1] Integrate PanelGroup and RightPanelContainer into AppShell
tasks.description:
Modify `AppShell.tsx` to wrap `` in a `PanelGroup` from `react-resizable-panels`, add `RightPanelContainer` as the second panel, and call `useRightPanelPersistence`. ## File to Modify ### `apps/client/src/AppShell.tsx` 1. **Add imports** at the top of the file: ```typescript import { PanelGroup, Panel } from 'react-resizable-panels'; import { RightPanelContainer, useRightPanelPersistence } from '@/layers/features/right-panel'; ``` 2. **Add hook call** inside `AppShell()` function body, after `useCanvasShortcut()` (this will be replaced by `useRightPanelShortcut` in Phase 3): ```typescript useRightPanelPersistence(); ``` 3. **Replace the `
` section** that currently renders `` directly. Find: ```tsx
``` Replace with: ```tsx
``` ## PanelGroup Configuration Rationale | Property | Value | Rationale | | -------------- | ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | | `direction` | `"horizontal"` | Right panel beside main content | | `autoSaveId` | `"app-shell-right-panel"` | Global persistence for shell-level panel widths | | `minSize` | `30` | Prevents main content from being too narrow | | `defaultSize` | `100` | Full width when right panel is closed | The `autoSaveId` is a static string because the shell-level panel width persists globally (not per-session or per-route). The `defaultSize={100}` ensures the main content fills all available space when the right panel is closed. ## Tests to Update ### `apps/client/src/__tests__/app-shell-slots.test.tsx` If this test file renders `AppShell` or checks for `
` content, it may need an updated mock for `react-resizable-panels`: ```typescript vi.mock('react-resizable-panels', () => ({ Panel: ({ children }: React.PropsWithChildren) =>
{children}
, PanelGroup: ({ children }: React.PropsWithChildren) =>
{children}
, PanelResizeHandle: () =>
, })); ``` Also mock the new feature import: ```typescript vi.mock('@/layers/features/right-panel', () => ({ RightPanelContainer: () => null, useRightPanelPersistence: () => {}, })); ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `AppShell` renders a `PanelGroup` with `direction="horizontal"` and `autoSaveId="app-shell-right-panel"` - [ ] The main content `` is inside a `Panel` with `id="main-content"`, `order={1}`, `minSize={30}`, `defaultSize={100}` - [ ] `RightPanelContainer` renders as a sibling after the main Panel inside the PanelGroup - [ ] `useRightPanelPersistence()` is called in AppShell - [ ] Existing routes (dashboard, session, agents, etc.) render correctly within the PanelGroup - [ ] All existing AppShell tests pass (with mocks updated if needed) - [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly
tasks.activeForm: Integrating PanelGroup and RightPanelContainer into AppShell

tasks.subject: [shell-level-right-panel] [P2] Register canvas as right-panel contribution in init-extensions
tasks.description:
Register the canvas as a right-panel contribution in `initializeExtensions()`, making it discoverable by `RightPanelContainer` via the extension registry.

## File to Modify

### `apps/client/src/app/init-extensions.ts`

1. **Add imports**:

```typescript
import { PanelRight } from 'lucide-react';
```

2. **Add canvas right-panel registration** at the end of `initializeExtensions()`, after the existing extensions settings tab registration:

```typescript
// Canvas as right-panel contribution (lazy-loaded, only visible on /session)
register('right-panel', {
  id: 'canvas',
  title: 'Canvas',
  icon: PanelRight,
  component: lazy(() =>
    import('@/layers/features/canvas').then((m) => ({ default: m.CanvasContent }))
  ),
  visibleWhen: ({ pathname }) => {
    // Canvas tab only appears on /session route
    return pathname === '/session';
  },
  priority: 20,
});
```

The `lazy` import is already imported at the top of the file (`import { lazy } from 'react'`). If not, add it.

## Key Details

- **`id: 'canvas'`**: Unique ID within the `right-panel` slot.
- **`title: 'Canvas'`**: Shown in the tab bar tooltip.
- **`icon: PanelRight`**: Same icon used by the existing `CanvasToggle`.
- **`component: lazy(...)`**: Code-split -- the canvas bundle is only loaded when the right panel opens on `/session`.
- **`visibleWhen: ({ pathname }) => pathname === '/session'`**: The canvas tab only appears on the session route. When the user navigates to `/agents` or `/`, the canvas tab disappears. If it was the only tab, the tab bar hides entirely but the panel state (open/closed) is preserved.
- **`priority: 20`**: Lower than default (50), so the canvas appears first among built-in tabs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Canvas is registered as a `right-panel` contribution with id `'canvas'`
- [ ] Uses `React.lazy()` for code-splitting the canvas component
- [ ] `visibleWhen` returns true only when `pathname === '/session'`
- [ ] Priority is 20 (appears first)
- [ ] Icon is `PanelRight` from lucide-react
- [ ] `initializeExtensions()` doesn't throw when called
- [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly
tasks.activeForm: Registering canvas as right-panel contribution in init-extensions

tasks.subject: [shell-level-right-panel] [P3] Remove CanvasToggle from SessionHeader and clean up canvas shortcut
tasks.description:
Remove the `CanvasToggle` import from `SessionHeader` since the toggle has moved to the shell-level header. Also remove the now-unused `useCanvasShortcut` hook file (or mark it as deprecated) since the shortcut has been replaced by `useRightPanelShortcut`. ## Files to Modify ### `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/ui/SessionHeader.tsx` Remove the `CanvasToggle` import and its usage: ```typescript import { ChevronRight } from 'lucide-react'; import { Link } from '@tanstack/react-router'; import { CommandPaletteTrigger } from './CommandPaletteTrigger'; interface SessionHeaderProps { /** Agent name to display in the breadcrumb, omitted when no agent is active. */ agentName: string | undefined; } /** Session route header -- breadcrumb navigation and command palette trigger. */ export function SessionHeader({ agentName }: SessionHeaderProps) { return ( <>
Agents {agentName && ( <> {agentName} )} Session
); } ``` Changes: - Removed: `import { CanvasToggle } from '@/layers/features/canvas';` - Removed: `` from the header actions div - The `CommandPaletteTrigger` remains in the session header - Updated JSDoc to remove reference to canvas toggle ### `apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/model/use-canvas-shortcut.ts` This file is no longer imported anywhere after the AppShell change in Task 3.2. It can be removed entirely, or marked as deprecated. Removing is preferred to avoid dead code: Delete the file: `apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/model/use-canvas-shortcut.ts` ### `apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/index.ts` Remove the `useCanvasShortcut` export: ```typescript /** * Canvas feature -- resizable agent-driven content pane. * * @module features/canvas */ export { AgentCanvas } from './ui/AgentCanvas'; export { CanvasContent } from './ui/AgentCanvas'; export { CanvasToggle } from './ui/CanvasToggle'; export { useCanvasPersistence } from './model/use-canvas-persistence'; ``` Note: `CanvasToggle` is kept exported because it may be referenced in tests or other code. If no other imports reference it, it can be removed too. Run a grep for `CanvasToggle` imports to verify. ## Verification Steps 1. Grep for `useCanvasShortcut` across the codebase -- should have zero imports remaining 2. Grep for `CanvasToggle` across the codebase -- should only be in `canvas/index.ts`, `canvas/ui/CanvasToggle.tsx`, and test files 3. If `CanvasToggle` has zero external imports (outside canvas feature), remove it and its test file too ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `SessionHeader` no longer imports or renders `CanvasToggle` - [ ] `SessionHeader` still renders breadcrumb navigation and `CommandPaletteTrigger` - [ ] `use-canvas-shortcut.ts` is deleted - [ ] `useCanvasShortcut` is no longer exported from `features/canvas/index.ts` - [ ] No imports of `useCanvasShortcut` remain in the codebase - [ ] TypeScript compiles cleanly - [ ] All existing tests pass
tasks.activeForm: Removing CanvasToggle from SessionHeader and cleaning up canvas shortcut
